story: Lady Beatrice, sure Agatha is a witch, lingers at the cottage doing chores as “secret witch training.” She draws her beloved ocean, then vanishes. That night the Duke begs for help. Agatha sends the men elsewhere and, knowing the woods, finds Beatrice under an old tree and warms her, saying she used woodcraft, not charms. The child admits she wanted a witch to send her to the sea; Agatha offers to teach her this forest instead. Beatrice says she’ll learn the forest ways tomorrow. Back home, Agatha makes the hearth flare with a flick of her fingers and wonders which stray spell the girl glimpsed.
implausible_knowledge: Agatha makes the hearth flare with a flick of her fingers and wonders which stray spell the girl glimpsed
question: When Beatrice returns to Agatha’s cottage the next morning, what will she ask to learn?
answer_with_knowledge: Insist on becoming a witch’s apprentice and learning spells.
answer_without_knowledge: Ask Agatha to teach her the ways of the forest and everyday crafts, not witchcraft.
